title: Golden-garlicky smoky Uruguayan penne pasta
description: A pescatarian-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring penne pasta, with a smoky, garlicky flavor and mustardy kick.
instructions: Cook the penne pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the smoked paprika and garlic powder, and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the Dijon mustard and anchovy paste to the skillet, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped parsley and crushed red pepper flakes to the skillet, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the reserved pasta water to the skillet, and stir to combine.
Add the cooked penne pasta to the skillet, and toss with the sauce until well combined.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Add the shrimp to the skillet, and cook until pink and fully cooked.
Transfer the pasta to a serving platter or individual plates. Top with grated Parmesan cheese, and serve immediately.
